mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs for corporations and consumers, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and lower corporate earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales and housing starts indicates a synchronized contraction in consumer and housing demand, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns with statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which supports short-term equity price appreciation over the next 5 trading days during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum yields negative 5-day returns in greater than 50 percent of observations during expansionary regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly increases in the CPIAUCSL index above 205.0 signal accelerating price pressures that force central banks to tighten policy, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in greater than 65 percent of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 205.0.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and a stable or declining unemployment rate indicates labor market tightening and wage inflation, which compresses profit margins and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields positive 20-day returns in greater than 55 percent of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP measured by GDPC1 reflects underlying economic expansion that supports corporate earnings trajectories, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 growth remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day returns are negative in greater than 50 percent of positive growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below 110.0, manufacturing output contraction signals weakening supply-side momentum and reduced business investment, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in greater than 60 percent of observations where INDPRO drops below 110.0.
